message:
fix(rpc): bound user-supplied netname/host/domain reads (bd-z4k96 class)

REVIEW round 5: four POSIX RPC entry points (host2netname,
netname2host, netname2user, user2netname) dereferenced caller-supplied
*const c_char pointers via CStr::from_ptr without a length bound. A
non-NUL-terminated host/domain/netname argument walked arbitrary
process memory until hitting a stray zero byte — same info-leak /
segfault class as inet_pton (commit 0c0b59f1) and execvp (commit
1d68447a).

Reachability: every Sun-RPC client/server that uses Secure RPC name
mapping (rpc.rusersd, NIS netname helpers, rpcbind authentication)
hits these entry points. An attacker-supplied or memory-corrupted
text pointer became an arbitrary-read primitive across the libc.so
boundary.

Fix: add the standard read_bounded_cstr helper (mirrors copies in
locale/iconv/dlfcn/inet/process — eventually candidate for a shared
util) that wraps scan_c_string + known_remaining; replace each
unbounded CStr::from_ptr on a user pointer with the bounded read; on
None, return the function's standard "invalid input" sentinel (0).

Full abi suite: 3120/3120 pass; 35/35 rpc_abi_test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
